title: Wazuh Alert Investigation
summary: Student investigates a Wazuh alert and documents triage, evidence, and disposition.
student_goal: Review a security alert, identify the affected asset and event details, and document whether it is benign, suspicious, or requires escalation.

required_evidence.label: Alert details
required_evidence.description: Student must provide alert title, rule ID, severity, and timestamp.

required_evidence.label: Affected asset
required_evidence.description: Student must identify the affected endpoint, user, or service.

required_evidence.label: Disposition
required_evidence.description: Student must classify the alert as benign, suspicious, or requiring escalation with reasoning.

opening_questions: What rule ID and severity triggered the alert?
Which host, user, or service is affected?
What evidence supports benign, suspicious, or escalation status?
mentor_guidance_rules: Do not close a security alert without evidence and disposition.
Ask for rule ID, severity, asset, and timestamp before conclusion.
Escalate if evidence suggests compromise or if impact is unclear.
